channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Lenovo Says Memory Price Hikes May Never Fully Reverse
item.content:encoded:
While it’s not the most surprising announcement, Lenovo has said that DRAM prices will not return to previous norms for a long time. In fact, it’s stated to be likely that they will never return to the prices that were standard just last year.

This announcement comes from a presentation at ISC 2026. Also, this falls at a time when gaming hardware is rapidly increasing in price, and Xbox just announced another price hike for consoles that have been on the market for several years now.

Lenovo Expects Memory Prices to Remain Elevated for Years

The comments from the Lenovo representative are taken from a ComputerBase.de report. It’s reported that the use of the word “never” when stating that prices won’t return to the previous year’s norm was exaggerated. However, it’s believed that for at least the next five years, these raised prices are expected.

“A ‘new normal’ could emerge from 2030 onwards, but with significantly higher prices than in 2024 and 2025. This already takes into account the large-scale launch of new manufacturing capacities starting in 2028,” the report states.

  • More Hardware News: PC RAM Prices Have Soared By Up To 250%

Earlier this week, Valve announced that its Steam Machine will cost over $1,000. Apple also recently announced unavoidable price hikes for its tech products. PlayStation, which recently outlined plans to go all in on AI, will reportedly launch its next console sooner than previously anticipated.

How do you feel about the rising cost of hardware? Has it led to impulse buys for you to try and get ahead of the game before the price increases? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server.

For more on Insider Gaming, learn about the recent issues affecting Star Wars Eclipse’s development. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: XBOX Reports ‘Record’ GTA 6 Orders And Says ‘People Should Wait For Real Data’
item.content:encoded:
Folks were turning feral recently based on a tidbit released by IGN, suggesting that PlayStation 5 pre-orders for Grand Theft Auto 6 were outpacing XBOX-based pre-orders by a whopping 8-to-1 ratio. However, the ‘data’ was based on the team’s internal affiliate systems, and not anything more concrete.

In a new report from Windows Central, an XBOX spokesperson has confirmed that the numbers published by IGN do not represent pre-order data, and that XBOX has had ‘record orders’ for the game. It wasn’t stressed in what capacity that record exists, though.

XBOX is Doing Just Fine, Apparently

In recent days, social media was ablaze with people running with a wild claim that PlayStation 5 pre-orders for GTA 6 were outgunning those on XBOX platforms by a massive margin. This fire was fuelled by IGN posting multiple times about the record, which caused netizens worldwide to spread the claims.

However, the whole thing didn’t add up too well, which meant that many outlets, including Insider Gaming, were reluctant to comment on the numbers.

In a new report from Windows Central, it was confirmed that XBOX has formally addressed the claims:

This doesn’t represent pre-order data. We’ve had record orders. People should wait for real data and not clicks on affiliate links.

Again, it isn’t clear in what capacity that record has been recorded. It could be the fastest that a game has reached a certain milestone, which is the most likely situation at play. The expectation is that Grand Theft Auto 6 will be the most successful game ever made in terms of day-one sales, and many believe it will recoup development costs in pre-orders alone.

  • RELATED: GTA 6 Takes Over PlayStation 5’s Home Screen

Windows Central went as far as to suggest IGN was partaking in the spreading of misinformation based on how it was handling the data available, and I’d certainly lean in the direction of agreeing with that.

It’s hardly a wild prediction to say that GTA 6 will sell better on PlayStation, especially considering it’s supposed to be the best platform for the game.

Do you think the margin is as wide as IGN claimed? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that a full, interactive GTA 6 map is being made, and be sure to sign up for our Insider Gaming newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Full GTA 6 Map Being Put Together on Interactive Site by Fans
item.content:encoded:
The latest round of attempts at recreating the Grand Theft Auto 6 map has surfaced online, courtesy of eagle-eyed fans scrutinizing every piece of GTA 6 media available. From the trailers to the screenshots, and from promotional materials to leaks, every inch of content is being scoured to assemble the most comprehensive build possible.

The result is an interactive map that features hundreds of points of interest, all picked out of the mass of imagery available online, both officially released and obtained through some more questionable sources over the years. It’s super impressive and goes to extreme lengths as a cataloguing effort.

Grand Theft Auto 6 Map Site Being Built

If you want the best idea possible of what Grand Theft Auto 6’s map looks like, you’ll want to head to map.stateofleonida.net. This is a fan-made resource that has taken every shred of GTA 6 data possible to assemble a universal best guess at the game’s full map.

Here’s a shot of the centre of the map, so you can get an idea of what I’m talking about:

It’s worth stressing that this resource has existed since at least March 2025, with various creators and tech-savvy individuals contributing to the upkeep of the site. The latest version of the map, maintained by someone named ‘YANIS’, is the thirteenth version of the map, published on June 24, mere days ago.

The latest version before that was from May 11, so this naturally incorporates everything from the dozens of new images uploaded by Rockstar Games to promote the Ultimate Edition of the game and all the pre-order content that’s coming soon.

It all goes much deeper, though. It’s not just roads and terrain that fans have mapped here, but also stores, points of interest, safehouses, activity centres, and much more. It also incorporates the game’s multiple biomes, including Mount Kalaga National Park, the swamplands, Port Gellhorn, and the city centre itself.

Have you played around with the GTA 6 map site yet? Let us know if you think it’s accurate on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that there are ‘no plans’ to ever print GTA 6 discs, and be sure to sign up for our Insider Gaming newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: All Esports World Cup 2025 Winners—Who Won at Last Year’s EWC?
item.content:encoded:
The Esports World Cup is upon us, and it’s set to make history. This supersized event has moved from Riyadh to Paris, and that promises that the footfall will be higher than ever before. It’s in the heart of Europe, and audiences are excited – more than ever before.

As we get into this action this year, we’ve provided a look back at last year’s results. Below, you’ll find a breakdown of every Esports World Cup 2025 winner, including which organization won the Club Championship portion of the tournament.

If you want to know who won at last year’s Esports World Cup, simply read on.

All The Winners From EWC 2025, Listed

Here’s the full spread of tournament winners from last year’s Esports World Cup, including what the prize pool was for that game.

  • Rennsport – Team Redline ($500,000)
  • VALORANT – Team Heretics ($1.25 million)
  • Dota 2 – Team Spirit ($3 million)
  • Fatal Fury: DetonatioN FocusMe ($1 million)
  • Apex Legends: VK Gaming ($2 million)
  • ML:BB Mid-Season Cup: Team Liquid ($3 million)
  • ML:BB Women’s Invitational: Team Vitality ($500,000)
  • Honor of Kings: AG Super Play ($3 million)
  • Free Fire: EVOS Divine ($1 million)
  • League of Legends: Gen.G ($2 million)
  • StarCraft II: Basilisk ($700,000)
  • Call of Duty: Black Ops 6: OpTic Gaming ($1.8 million)
  • PUBG Mobile: Yangon Galacticos ($3 million)
  • Chess: Team Liquid ($1.5 million)
  • Overwatch: Team Falcons ($1 million)
  • Rainbow Six Siege: Team Secret ($2 million)
  • Call of Duty: Warzone: Twisted Minds ($1 million)
  • EA Sports FC 25: Team Liquid ($1 million)
  • Teamfight Tactics: Weibo Gaming ($500,000)
  • PUBG: Twisted Minds ($2 million)
  • Tekken 8: FREECS ($1 million)
  • Rocket League: Karmine Corp ($1 million)
  • Crossfire: All Gamers ($2 million)
  • Street Fighter 6: KuaiShou Gaming ($1 million)
  • Counter-Strike 2: The MongolZ ($1.25 million)
  • Fancy yourself an esports expert? Start betting with a $20 deposit at Polymarket and receive a $50 bonus!

And here are the top organizations that made it to the highest echelons of the Club Championship in 2025, earning a spot in the 2026 Club Program at the Esports World Cup in Paris:

  1. Team Falcons ($7 million)
  2. Team Liquid ($4 million)
  3. Team Vitality ($3 million)
  4. Twisted Minds ($2.25 million)
  5. Virus.pro ($2.25 million)
  6. All Gamers International ($1.5 million)
  7. Gen.G Esports ($1 million)
  8. Weibo Gaming ($850,000)

This year’s tournament promises to be even more impressive than the first two. Are you headed to Paris to check out the action live at the Esports World Cup? Let us know which game you’re most looking forward to on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out our full breakdown of the EWC 2026 schedule, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For June 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, June 27, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Collection of solid particles in a liquid or gas
  • Hint 2: Found naturally or in a pressurized dispenser
  • Hint 3: Examples include fog, mist, hairspray, and deodorant

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Paint
  • Perfume
  • Scent
  • Nozzle
  • Gel
  • Lotion
  • Liquid
  • Mist
  • Spray

Today’s Contexto Answer For June 27, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Aerosol

An Aerosol is a mixture of particles and the liquid or gas that they are contained in, that can spread through the air.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on June 28.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: There Are ‘No Plans’ To Ever Print GTA 6 Discs
item.content:encoded:
Alongside price announcements for Grand Theft Auto 6, it was revealed that physical copies of the game would only include a download code inside the case, with no disc provided at launch.

Early reports suggested that this would only be a temporary measure, with a disc release for the game scheduled for December 2026. However, it’s now been revealed that there are currently ‘no plans’ for GTA 6 discs to be printed.

GTA 6 Will Not Receive Discs Afterall

Thanks to The Hollywood Reporter, we’ve been given an insight into the physical media debacle for Grand Theft Auto 6, and it seems like it’s a sorry conclusion.

The confusion stems from an email response to fans inquiring about a GTA 6 disc, which stated that “you will be able to acquire a physical copy during the following months.” That, alongside a report by ppe.pl, gave fans hope that a disc version of the game could arrive shortly after launch, but it seems like that’s far from the truth.

  • Related: Everything Included In GTA 6 Ultimate Edition & Price

According to a source with knowledge of the plans in place for GTA 6’s release, the language in the email is being misinterpreted. The use of the phrase “physical copy” simply refers to the physical version of the game, as in the case with the download code inside.

As for the “following months,” that’s reportedly just a reference to the months following Wednesday’s price and edition announcements. To round it off, the source simply states that there are no plans for GTA 6 discs to be printed, “not at launch, and not months after.”

It’s a sorry state of affairs for fans of physical media, since a lack of discs means no selling the game once you’re done and no trading or sharing with friends and family, all whilst providing further power to the digital storefronts on the console.

Will it stop people from buying GTA 6, though? Probably not, since the hype for this game far exceeds anything the industry may have seen before. Check out the trailer for yourself down below, and remind yourself why we’re all excited.

Would you have bought GTA 6 physically, had it come with a disc? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, read about the latest regarding GTA 6’s Collector’s Edition, and be sure to sign up for our Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion
